summary:
There is currently no evidence linking arginine vasopressin V1a receptor microsatellites to hypersexuality in children with prepubertal or early adolescent bipolar disorder. Clinical understanding attributes hypersexual behavior during mania mainly to dysregulation in dopaminergic and serotonergic pathways, not to variations in vasopressin receptor genes.  

\boxed{B}

Here is the final summary:

There is currently no evidence linking arginine vasopressin V1a receptor microsatellites to hypersexuality in children with prepubertal or early adolescent bipolar disorder. Hypersexuality in these patients is more plausibly associated with dopaminergic and serotonergic pathway dysfunction than with vascular pressor hormone receptor variants.
